description: Stackability (A5): non-stackable membership/subscription credits count once per portfolio; stackable statement credits count per card; an un-opted-in credit contributes nothing.

scenarios.name: Credit Stackability - Non-Stackable Credit Counted Once
scenarios.description: Two cards both carry Airport Lounge (stackable: false, different values). The higher-value card should get the full credit; the other gets a $0 info line naming it, and both headlines still reconcile.

test_conditions: Lounge Card High ($550 lounge value) wins the dedup
Lounge Card Low gets a $0 info line naming Lounge Card High
Both cards' line items still reconcile to their headline

scenarios.name: Credit Stackability - Stackable Credit Counted On Every Card
scenarios.description: Two cards both carry Uber Eats (stackable: true, different values). Both should count in full — no dedup, no info lines.

test_conditions: Uber Eats Card A counts its full $180/year ($15 x 12)
Uber Eats Card B counts its full $240/year ($20 x 12)
Neither card gets an info line — stackable credits never dedup

scenarios.name: Credit Stackability - No Preference Zeroes The Credit
scenarios.description: User never opted into Airport Lounge, so it contributes nothing to the card's value (an absent preference row, same as an explicit opt-out).

test_conditions: Lounge Card Low's breakdown has no Airport Lounge credit or info line
